message:
fix(channel): auto-flush with ownership tracking returns triggering message on failure

Replaces the previous auto-flush strategy (buffer-then-best-effort-flush)
with a new auto_flush_including_current method that tracks ownership of
each message through the flush pipeline using an AutoFlushItem enum that
distinguishes Buffered (previously queued) from Current (the message that
triggered the flush).

When delivery fails mid-flush (Disconnected, Cancelled, or Full), the
method extracts and returns the Current message to the caller via the
appropriate SendError variant, while re-queuing any undelivered Buffered
messages back to the reorder buffer via an RAII AutoFlushGuard. This
prevents the previous behavior where the triggering message could be
silently consumed into the buffer on failure, making it impossible for
the caller to retry or propagate the error with the original value.

The AutoFlushGuard's Drop implementation ensures that any messages still
in the pending queue (both Buffered and Current) are restored to the
reorder buffer if the future is cancelled or panics, preventing message
loss during unwinding.

Tests added:
- auto_flush_returns_disconnected_for_triggering_message: verifies the
  triggering value is returned in SendError::Disconnected when receiver
  drops during flush
- auto_flush_returns_cancelled_for_triggering_message: verifies the
  triggering value is returned in SendError::Cancelled when Cx is
  cancelled during flush

Also adds a test_waker() helper using a no-op Wake implementation for
manual poll-based test scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
